h2h_summary: Mexico dominate the all-time head-to-head with approximately 8 wins to Korea Republic's 4 across 13 meetings, including two World Cup victories in 1998 and 2018.

notable_results: Mexico 3-1 Korea Republic - FIFA World Cup Group Stage - 1998
Korea Republic 1-2 Mexico - FIFA World Cup Group Stage - 2018
Mexico 2-1 Korea Republic - FIFA World Cup Group Stage - 2026

wc_history_home: Mexico have appeared at 16 World Cups, co-hosting in 1970 and 1986 when they reached the quarter-finals — their best-ever performance — and are notorious for being eliminated in the Round of 16 seven consecutive times from 1994 to 2018.
wc_history_away: Korea Republic's greatest World Cup moment came in 2002 on home soil, when they became the first Asian team to reach the semi-finals, defeating Portugal, Italy, and Spain before losing to Germany 1-0.
group_context: Group A features co-hosts Mexico, Korea Republic, Czechia, and South Africa, with all matches played in Mexico giving El Tri a massive home advantage; Mexico are strong favourites to top the group while Korea Republic and Czechia battle for the second automatic qualifying spot in what is the most consequential second-game fixture for both sides.
strengths_home: Home crowd advantage and altitude at Estadio Akron in Guadalajara
Santiago Gimenez's elite goal-scoring form at AC Milan
Proven World Cup pedigree against Korea Republic (2 wins in 2 WC meetings)
strengths_away: Son Heung-min's world-class individual quality and leadership as captain
Kim Min-jae providing a formidable defensive foundation from Bayern Munich
Unbeaten Asian qualifying campaign with a disciplined vertical pressing system
team_style_home: Mexico under Javier Aguirre deploy a flexible 4-3-3 built around high-energy pressing, quick transitions, and Santiago Gimenez's pace up front, anchored by Edson Alvarez as the single pivot.
team_style_away: Korea Republic under Hong Myung-bo use a 4-2-3-1 with high-intensity pressing and vertical passing, leveraging Son Heung-min's creativity and pace on the left wing to unlock defences rapidly.
recent_news: South Korea trained as a full squad for the first time since arriving in their World Cup base camp in western Mexico, with injured players Kim Tae-hyeon and Bae Jun-ho returning to training. Mexico's national team faces a demanding group-stage match against South Korea in the FIFA World Cup 2026.
context: Group A features co-hosts Mexico, Korea Republic, Czechia, and South Africa, with all matches played in Mexico giving El Tri a massive home advantage; Mexico are strong favourites to top the group while Korea Republic and Czechia battle for the second automatic qualifying spot in what is the most consequential second-game fixture for both sides.
